name: openstaad-mcp
description: MCP server for Bentley STAAD.Pro via the OpenSTAAD API
long_description:
The OpenSTAAD MCP server enables Claude to interact with your STAAD.Pro models and perform various time-consuming tasks like load cases definition, data extraction, repetitive property setting and more.

## Key Features
- **Fast and flexible**: Enjoy minimal latency, interact with every STAAD.Pro features covered by the OpenSTAAD API.
- **AI-friendly**: Provides documentation, guidance and feedback via dedicated tools to help your AI agent ramp up quickly on the STAAD.Pro API.
- **Multi-instance support**: Connects to multiple running STAAD.Pro instances simultaneously to parallelize tasks across models.
- **Privacy-first**: All processing happens locally on your machine. No data is sent to the cloud. No telemetry.

This server requires having STAAD.Pro installed locally and running. If you don't already have it, download [STAAD.Pro (www.bentley.com)](https://www.bentley.com/software/staad) to get started.

Terms: Your use of this connector is subject to the [Bentley Systems Terms of Use (www.bentley.com)](https://www.bentley.com/legal/web-properties-terms-of-use).

tools.name: discover_api
tools.description: Discover available API guidance and skills. Call this before using other openstaad-mcp tools to understand the API surface and see what skills are available. Then use ``read_skills`` with one or more specific skill names to load full guidance.

tools.name: read_skills
tools.description: Read one or more skills by name. Use ``discover_api`` first to list available skills. Each skill provides domain-specific guidance (e.g. analysis, geometry, loads). Pass skill names like ``['staad-analysis']`` or sub-paths like ``['staad-steel-design/assets/DESIGN_CODES']`` to read reference files within a skill.

tools.name: list_instances
tools.description: List currently active STAAD instances. Returns instance IDs, model paths, and STAAD versions. Use instance IDs to target specific models in other tools.

tools.name: get_status
tools.description: Check the connection to STAAD.Pro. Returns connection state, STAAD version, model path, and analysis status.

tools.name: execute_code
tools.description: Execute Python code against the OpenSTAAD API. Imports are blocked; sandbox provides staad, json, math, progress. IMPORTANT: always call progress(f'Processing {i}/{total}') in loops with >20 elements and before long operations — without it the user sees NO feedback. Timeout and mode are auto-detected from code keywords (120s sync, 600s async for loops, 3600s async for analysis/design). Override with timeout= for precise control (e.g. elements×0.005 for large loops at ~3ms/call). Override with mode='async' to force background execution — returns {job_id}, poll with get_job_result.

tools.name: get_job_result
tools.description: IMPORTANT: always write the returned `message` field to the user in your text response so they see progress — it is the ONLY way they see updates. Returns current job status immediately. Check `strategy`: 'poll' → write message to user, wait `retry_after_seconds`, then call again; 'await_user_trigger' → stop polling, tell user job is still running, wait for their request.
